frames: The agent collapses forward, extending its front limbs while its rear limbs bend, bringing its body close to the ground.
The agent lies flat on the ground with all limbs bent, appearing to be in a resting or collapsed state.
The agent begins to push up with its front limbs, lifting the anterior part of its body while the rear remains low.
The agent achieves a more upright posture, balancing on its front limbs with its rear limbs bent and elevated.
The agent collapses forward again, lowering its body to the ground as its front limbs bend.
The agent is once more lying flat on the ground, with all its limbs bent and in contact with the surface.
The agent initiates another push-up motion, extending its front limbs to lift its upper body.
The agent reaches an upright position on its front limbs, with its rear limbs bent and off the ground, before slightly lowering.
